About.introText1: Pax Christi Korea (PCK) is the Korean branch of Pax Christi International (PCI), an international Catholic peace movement founded in 1945. PCK was established in 2019.
About.introText2: PCI is an unofficial Catholic peace movement organization where all members of the Catholic Church participate equally and horizontally in pursuing peace and reconciliation. Currently, more than 120 organizations in over 50 countries worldwide are working together. Based on the Gospel and Catholic faith, PCI pursues the vision of 'building a peaceful world, a world free from all violence' with prayer, study (research), and practice as its methodological principles.
About.introText3: At the global, continental, and national levels, PCI member organizations respond to regional or global challenges through independent or collaborative efforts—promoting nonviolent practices, improving human rights, enhancing human security, promoting disarmament and demilitarization, establishing a just international order, and achieving interfaith peace. PCI participates in international organizations such as the UN, UNESCO, the African Union, and the Council of Europe as a representative.
About.introText4: PCK welcomes the participation of all Catholic faithful. Membership is open to individuals—laity, religious, bishops, and priests alike. If you agree with our mission, please apply for individual membership.
About.visionDescription: In step with Pax Christi International, we actively develop peace movements that address Korean realities, pursuing the vision of 'building a peaceful world, a world free from all violence.'
About.missionDescription: As an unofficial Catholic peace movement where all members participate equally and horizontally in pursuing peace and reconciliation, we act based on the Gospel and Catholic faith, with prayer, study (research), and practice as our methodological principles.
About.activitiesSubtitle: We are pioneering peace movement areas suited to Korean realities in step with PCI
About.actConflictDesc: We work to prevent violent conflicts and propose alternative solutions when conflicts are ongoing. We mediate ethnic conflicts and religious divisions, visit conflict areas, and conduct peace campaigns.
About.actPeaceBuildingDesc: We pursue peace building that helps realize reconciliation and peace based on truth and justice.
About.actEducationDesc: We support peace education and youth activities including youth exchanges, alternative peace service, voluntary peace activities, and Peace Work weeks.
About.actNonviolentDesc: We organize various meetings and discussion groups to pursue social change through nonviolent methods.
About.actInterfaithDesc: We mediate religious divisions and create opportunities for interfaith dialogue and cooperation for peace.
About.actAdvocacyDesc: We participate in advocacy activities unique to each country and suited to its socio-political context.
About.actPartnerDesc: We facilitate exchanges between partner organizations and provide resources to enhance capacities and increase the effectiveness of activities.
About.actPeaceDayDesc: We reflect on and practice the Pope's World Peace Day messages and work to spread them within the Church.
